scope: Spec at .omc/specs/deep-dive-audit-the-entire-kids-account.md (from 3-lane deep-dive trace at .omc/specs/deep-dive-trace-audit-the-entire-kids-account.md). Six stories US-1..US-6 covering: parent-only onboarding fork UI, parent-mode dashboard, post-onboarding add/edit/remove kid, rich kid stats parity with parent's own dashboard, kid photos + parent waiver signing, LoginEvent FK drift hygiene. Schema + API plumbing for parent accountType already shipped in commit c49a997 (migration 20260512000001 + PATCH /api/member/me extensions).

stories.title: Parent-only onboarding fork UI
acceptanceCriteria: Step 0 of the onboarding modal in app/member/home/page.tsx shows two options: 'I train at this gym' and 'I'm here to manage my child' — replacing the current single 'Let\'s Go' button
Picking the parent-only option sets parentOnly=true and routes Next from Step 0 directly to Step 5 (kids), skipping Steps 1-4 (belt, classes, style, heard)
Step 6 (emergency contact) + Step 7 (waiver) still run for parent-only mode — parent has on-premises liability when collecting their child
Back-button from Step 5 returns to Step 0 (not Step 4) when parentOnly=true; existing training-flow back behaviour preserved when parentOnly=false
On finish(), PATCH /api/member/me body includes accountType: 'parent' when parentOnly=true; belt/stripes fields omitted entirely
Existing training-flow regression: a non-parentOnly user still walks Steps 1->7 in order without behaviour change

Unit test tests/unit/onboarding-parent-mode.test.tsx renders the modal, exercises the parent fork, asserts belt/style/heard steps are not reachable, and asserts the finish() fetch body for /api/member/me includes accountType: 'parent'
npx tsc --noEmit clean on modified files
npm run build exit 0

stories.title: Parent-only dashboard mode on /member/home
acceptanceCriteria: MemberHomePage in app/member/home/page.tsx reads accountType from /api/member/me

When accountType === 'parent', the 'Your next class' / personal-stats / 'Sign In to Class' (for self) widgets are replaced by a 'Your kids' feed listing each kid with: name, age (if DOB known), belt, next class for that kid, recent attendance summary
When accountType !== 'parent', the existing /member/home layout renders byte-identical to before US-2 (no regression for training members)
The Sign-In sheet kid picker continues to work for both modes (Session E commit 2fd22e9 unchanged)
Unit/integration smoke test added that mounts /member/home with accountType='parent' fixture and asserts the kid feed renders + the 'Your next class' personal widget does NOT render
npx tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build exit 0

stories.title: Post-onboarding add / edit / remove kid (UI + PATCH endpoint)
acceptanceCriteria: components/member/FamilySection.tsx renders a '+ Add child' button (replacing the 'contact billing@...' empty-state copy at lines 79-96)
components/member/EditChildModal.tsx exists and is used for both Create (from FamilySection) and Edit (from kid row '...' menu)
Each kid row in FamilySection gains a '...' menu offering Edit (name + DOB) and Remove (calls existing DELETE /api/member/children/[id])
app/api/member/children/[id]/route.ts gains a PATCH handler that accepts ONLY { name?: string, dateOfBirth?: string } in the Zod body — any other field is silently dropped, never written
PATCH guard: composite predicate { id: childId, tenantId, parentMemberId: session.memberId } used at every step (single findFirst; no TOCTOU)
PATCH returns 200 + updated kid on success, 404 when the kid is not the caller's child (cross-parent attempt), 400 on invalid input

Test extension in tests/integration/member-children-lifecycle.test.ts: PATCH rename succeeds; cross-parent PATCH returns 404 + DB row unchanged; PATCH with {status: 'cancelled'} returns 200 but status in DB unchanged
npx tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build exit 0; new + extended tests load cleanly via npx vitest run

stories.title: Rich kid stats parity with parent's own dashboard
acceptanceCriteria: New lib/member-stats.ts exports computeMemberStats(tx, { memberId, tenantId }) returning { thisWeek, thisMonth, thisYear, streakWeeks, totalClasses, attendanceByClass, avgClassesPerWeek, nextClass }
app/api/member/me/route.ts is refactored to call computeMemberStats — the response JSON shape is unchanged (parity with current callers) but the implementation is now a single helper call
app/api/member/children/[id]/route.ts GET response is extended with the same stats object
app/member/family/[childId]/page.tsx renders the new stats (thisWeek / streak / nextClass) next to the existing belt + waiver tiles

New test tests/integration/member-children-stats.test.ts seeds a kid with at least 3 attendance rows across 2 ISO weeks, calls the kid GET, and asserts the shape matches the parent's own /api/member/me stats response
npx tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build exit 0; new test loads cleanly via npx vitest run

stories.title: Kid photos (evidence) + parent waiver signing
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma defines a new MemberPhoto model with fields { id, tenantId, memberId (FK Member, ON DELETE CASCADE), url (String), caption (String?), kind (CHECK: 'evidence'|'milestone'|'promotion'), uploadedAt, uploadedByMemberId (FK Member?, ON DELETE SET NULL) } and indexes (memberId, uploadedAt) + (tenantId)

Migration prisma/migrations/<timestamp>_member_photos/migration.sql creates the table, the FKs (with CASCADE on memberId, SET NULL on uploadedByMemberId), the indexes, the CHECK on kind, and an RLS policy that mirrors the existing Member table tenant_isolation policy

app/api/member/children/[id]/photos/route.ts: POST (parent uploads — calls existing /api/upload internal pattern; accepts data: URL fallback up to ~3M chars), GET (parent fetches own kid's photos, ordered uploadedAt DESC)
app/api/member/children/[id]/photos/[photoId]/route.ts: DELETE (parent removes own kid's photo). Composite predicate guards: kid is parent's child AND photo.memberId === kid.id

app/api/waiver/sign-for-child/route.ts: parent signs the kid's waiver. Mirrors /api/waiver/sign body shape, creates SignedWaiver row with memberId=kid.id and collectedBy=parent.memberId. 404 when kid isn't caller's child
app/member/family/[childId]/page.tsx renders a photo grid with '+ Add Photo' CTA, and (when child.waiverAccepted=false) a 'Sign waiver for [child]' button that opens an inline signature flow

Cascade test tests/integration/member-children-photos.test.ts: parent uploads -> row exists; cross-parent upload -> 404; kid deletion via lib/member-delete.ts removes the photo row automatically (proves ON DELETE CASCADE FK)
Photo GET visibility: only the parent of the kid AND staff in the same tenant can read; cross-family attempt -> 404
npx tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build exit 0

stories.title: Hygiene: LoginEvent FK drift fix
acceptanceCriteria: Confirmed via prisma db pull or direct psql whether LoginEvent has FK constraints on userId + memberId in the live Neon test branch
If FK constraints missing: new migration <timestamp>_login_event_fk_alignment/migration.sql adds them with ON DELETE CASCADE matching prisma/schema.prisma lines 418, 420
lib/member-delete.ts header comment corrected to match schema (was claiming SET NULL; schema says CASCADE)
lib/member-delete.ts adds explicit tx.loginEvent.deleteMany({ where: { memberId } }) before the final member.deleteMany so the helper is correct regardless of FK presence
tests/integration/member-cascade-delete.test.ts extended to seed a LoginEvent row for the test member, run DELETE, and assert no LoginEvent row remains
npx tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build exit 0
